mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent economic contraction, causing investors to discount future corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect accelerating manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases do not precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns during early-cycle expansions, with a win rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 220.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 220.0 are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and total nonfarm payrolls confirms synchronized economic expansion, which boosts corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive co-movement between GDPC1 and PAYEMS yields negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand household income and consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day S&P 500 forward returns across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 95.0 while the consumer price index remains above 220.0, supply constraints combine with persistent inflation to erode corporate margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined condition of INDPRO below 95.0 and CPIAUCSL above 220.0 fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 50%.
